title: Using a Grill Mat for Making Easy Outdoor Grilled Cheese
keyword: grilling cheese on non stick mat
meta_description: Discover how to grill cheese on a non‑stick mat for the perfect outdoor grilled cheese. Learn tips, tricks, and why BBQ Grill Mats are a game‑changer.
html:
When you pull out the grill for a weekend lunch, the first thing that comes to mind is often burgers or hot dogs. But grilling cheese on a non stick mat opens up a whole new world of quick, melty sandwiches that are crisp on the outside and gooey on the inside. Using a grill mat not only prevents the cheese from slipping through the grates, it also eliminates flare‑ups and makes cleanup a breeze.

Why a Grill Mat Beats Plain Grates for Grilling Cheese on Non Stick Mat

Cheese is notorious for melting quickly and then sliding into the gaps of a traditional grill. A non‑stick grill mat creates a flat, even surface that keeps the cheese where you want it. The mat’s heat‑resistant silicone coating distributes heat uniformly, so you get a golden crust without burning the cheese underneath. Because the mat is reusable, you can keep cooking delicate foods without worrying about disposable foil or parchment.

Choosing the Right Grill Mat for Your Sandwich

Not all grill mats are created equal. Look for a mat that is:

  • Food‑grade silicone, BPA‑free, and safe for temperatures up to at least 500°F.
  • Large enough to cover your grill’s cooking area, ensuring the cheese never sees a bare grate.
  • Easy to clean – most mats are dishwasher safe, but a quick hand wash works too.

The product we recommend, BBQ Grill Mats, meets all these criteria. Their non‑stick surface is specifically engineered for delicate foods, making them perfect for grilled cheese, fish, and veggies.

Step‑By‑Step: How to Grill Cheese on a Non Stick Mat

Follow this simple routine and you’ll have a perfectly crisp grilled cheese in under ten minutes.

  1. Preheat the grill. Light your charcoal or turn on the gas burners and set the temperature to medium‑high (about 400°F). Let the grill heat for 10‑15 minutes.
  2. Lay the mat. Place the BBQ Grill Mat directly on the grates. It will start to glow slightly as it reaches cooking temperature.
  3. Butter the bread. Lightly spread softened butter on one side of each slice of bread. This creates a barrier that helps the bread brown evenly.
  4. Assemble the sandwich. Place the buttered side down on the mat, add your cheese (cheddar, mozzarella, or a blend), then top with the second slice, butter side up.
  5. Close the lid. Keep the grill covered for the first 2‑3 minutes to melt the cheese, then open the lid to check the crust. Flip the sandwich if you want an even brown on both sides.
  6. Remove and rest. When the bread is golden and the cheese is oozy, lift the sandwich off the mat with a spatula. Let it sit a minute before cutting – the cheese will settle and won’t spill out.

Because the mat prevents direct contact with the flames, you’ll avoid those dreaded char spots that can turn a simple grilled cheese into a burnt mess.

Tips for Elevating Your Outdoor Grilled Cheese

Once you’ve mastered the basics, experiment with these ideas to keep the sandwich exciting:

  • Add a crunch. Sprinkle a thin layer of crushed crackers or toasted breadcrumbs on the cheese before closing the sandwich.
  • Flavor boosters. A dash of garlic powder, smoked paprika, or a drizzle of honey can transform the flavor profile.
  • Mix cheeses. Combine sharp cheddar with creamy brie for a balance of melt and bite.
  • Include extras. Thin slices of apple, caramelized onions, or roasted red peppers add texture and sweetness.

All of these toppings stay in place on the non‑stick mat, so you won’t lose any ingredients through the grill’s gaps.

Cleaning and Maintaining Your Grill Mat

After the meal, let the BBQ Grill Mat cool completely before handling it. Most residues lift off with a warm, soapy sponge. For stubborn bits, a quick soak in warm water does the trick. Because the mat is silicone‑based, it won’t warp or crack in the dishwasher, but hand‑washing prolongs its lifespan.

Store the mat flat or rolled in a dry place. Avoid folding it sharply, as repeated creases can eventually weaken the material. With proper care, a single mat can last for many seasons of grilled cheese, fish tacos, and vegetable medleys.

FAQ

Can I use a grill mat on a charcoal grill?

Yes. The mat is heat‑resistant up to 500°F, which covers most charcoal grilling temperatures. Just place it directly on the hot grates after the coals have settled.

Will the grill mat affect the flavor of my cheese?

No. The silicone coating is inert and does not release any chemicals or flavors. It simply provides a non‑stick surface, letting the cheese’s natural taste shine.

Is the BBQ Grill Mat safe for direct contact with food?

Absolutely. It is made from food‑grade silicone, BPA‑free, and approved for use at high temperatures, making it safe for any direct cooking application.
